binding_constraint_top1: A systemic governance-and-capability failure at high-impact auditees — especially weak consequences, poor procurement/contract control, and inadequate oversight — rather than a lack of mandates or funding.
binding_constraint_top3: Inadequate institutional capability to produce credible reporting and comply with law.
Governance failures and weak consequences that normalize non-compliance.
Weak oversight and intergovernmental coordination, especially where service delivery depends on multiple institutions.
slow_variables: Professionalisation of systems and controls in high-impact entities
contributing_factors: High-impact auditees remained persistently weak despite repeated prior warnings.
Procurement and contract-management failures drove large irregular-expenditure totals.
Many institutions relied on the audit process itself to detect and correct material misstatements.
The report states that roleplayers in the accountability ecosystem were not fulfilling their legislative roles consistently or effectively.
good_interventions_ranked: Target high-impact auditees with accountable remediation plans focused on governance, controls, procurement, and credible reporting.
Strengthen consequence management and independent oversight so repeated non-compliance has visible operational consequences.
Improve institutional capability and systems in the areas that drive service delivery risk: procurement, contract management, reporting, and cross-government delivery planning.
bad_interventions: Adding generic training or funding without accountability and follow-through
Focusing on communication or presentation of audit results instead of high-impact control failures
early_warning_signals: Large auditees repeatedly remaining unqualified-with-findings or regressing
Irregular expenditure under prolonged review with no closure
Material misstatements corrected only after audit intervention
Repeat procurement deviations such as evergreen contracts and month-to-month extensions
distractors_to_penalize: Vague calls for 'more resources' without control reform
Celebrating small aggregate improvements while high-impact entities remain weak
Short-term optics measures that leave procurement and oversight unchanged
evidence: The Auditor-General identified three main shortcomings: governance failures/lack of consequences and integrity, inadequate institutional capability, and weaknesses in oversight and intergovernmental coordination.
The report also states these weaknesses are not mainly due to unclear mandates or insufficient funding.
